item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here and is copyrighted. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scorpion is right, but being hijacked as an insult isn't the only reason for the change - I.D. is also more precise term.

"Retardation" means a delay or inhibition of growth/development (which, because of the hijacked-as-insult thing, we now refer to as "developmental delays" or "developmental impairment"). People with intellectual disabilities tend to also have a developmental issue as the underlying cause (for example, Down's syndrome is a developmental condition that's highly comorbid with intellectual disability). However, there are all kinds of ways a person can be developmentally typical and still end up intellectually disabled, and people with developmental conditions aren't always intellectually disabled (for example, plenty of people with Down's syndrome aren't). So clinicians have separated the terms, in addition to changing the one that's become an insult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not anymore; the linguistic treadmill has moved that one into the "hijacked as an insult" realm. The favored replacement is Intellectual Disability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"mental retardation" is actually the clinical term, AFAIK.

edit: Well, I stand corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if it counts as a minor edit or not, but I would like to propose updating "some form of mental retardation" at the beginning of this article to "some form of intellectual disability."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably the doctor wrote it down or made some other form of reliable report soon afterward. From there one could confirm it via lip reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the security cameras did not hold the conversation, why is there a transcript?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. This pushes a major fear button of mine—in the downstairs bathroom, there's a shower, and the toilet is in the far side of the bathroom, away from the door, such that if someone jumped out from behind the shower curtain, they could block me from escaping. As such, I have this irrational fear that while I'm taking a piss, while noone is downstairs because it's the middle of the night, that some crazy, knife/gun wielding person will just manifest out of nowhere behind the shower curtain and jump out and stab/shoot me. When I was younger, that person was Voldemort instead, because of that instant death curse that he could use that involves shooting green light out of his wand and that I forget the words to. That's why I pull the curtain back so I can see into the shower while I'm taking a piss so that they can't manifest because they have nowhere hidden to manifest.

Weeewh, wall of text DX

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is an old skip, but I feel like I should comment because this reminds me of something that actually happened to me. As crazy as that sounds.

Needless to say, it made this all the more creepy and chilling for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "sony vhs tape of average make" is a bad line. If you mean it is nothing special probably better to just say "sony vhs tape".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Down voted for an absolutely HUGE image.

Might be my phone, but the image takes in excess of thirty seconds to load. Pictures on other articles take less than a second or two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In-universe, where did the article picture come from? That's a high-resolution image of the eye of a man who only appears on VHS footage. Did they borrow image enhancement software from CSI?

item.title: Re: Question...
item.content:encoded: ~It is a mystery~

item.title: Question...
item.content:encoded: If it was recovered in France, why is "Just Curious" written on it in English?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why not let this SCP to "view" a cadaver dissetion with an interested D-class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. Could be better, but pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know who Gareth is, but he sounds pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the eye makeup made me immediately think of Tim Minchin. And imagining that made this article that much more creepy. 0.o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My little porny, my little porny…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like that scene in the UK version of the Office where people are joking about porn and then Gareth shows up to share his extensive knowledge and interest on the subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you have a Pixiv account and a web translator you can see some of the 'BEST' horrifyingly realistic MLP porn, that includes but is not limited to…. well I would list it but there's just so much and it's all a different shade of repulsive….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, this is pretty good, and unfortunately may keep me from enacting an idea I've had for a while. This is just really well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thats because the part of your brain that is shocked by the internet is hardened and calloused from overuse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Horror

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it more hilarious than hot. Especially the vocabulary used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've already seen MLP porn, it's more weird than scary.

Why does it exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<.<

I dunno man, those G3 and G3.5 cartoons and movies are awfully scary…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've heard people say "I want to watch My Little Pony porn" before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First SCP? I don't believe that. You did an outstanding job at creeping me out! There are some very minor tone issues here and there, but I think everyone else has noted most of them already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall, it's an alright idea. The interview is interesting enough to make me upvote, though, as well as the history. My suggestion would be to fix up the tone in the section saying why the killer hasn't been affected yet. It feels too casual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean "I want to watch My Little Pony" isn't the scariest thing you've ever heard?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote changed to upvote after the edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the fair comments, everyone. Changes made as I saw fit and you suggested. The problem, I believe, was that I was too clear on parts that should be shrouded and too obscure in places it was unnecessary.

A few pin-point questions: Event-1757-SE specifically refers to being stalked by the Curious Man, not just the attack - is this clear from the text? Watching the film places no unconquerable trance or suggestion on a person to watch; what drives them over the edge is their own damned curiosity - should this be emphasized more?

I love that some people picked up the two-way curiosity link: the SCP reflects the interest taken towards it, and if the connection grows strong enough it can leave the casing to "study" the observer closer. I do know that men wear mascara as well, the text was meant to accent the fact that it was feminine (the picture is of a woman for this very reason). Removed for distracting from the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it weird that a man wearing mascara is what made me upvote? Solid article otherwise, but that image pushes it into unsettling territory. It's distinctive, almost iconic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A few of the problems with tone have already been mentioned, as has the somewhat unoriginal nature of the concept, but overall the execution is very good and quite subtle. The interview with 1757-A is solid, but I honestly think the article would work better if you dropped it, and scrubbed all mention of an entity deriving from the tape; you imply everything you need to with The Strangler's typo and his "immunity", and it works better as a (small) mystery. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the interview packed a nice creeper punch, that last addendum ruins it again. Apart from that, as others have said, it's a movie that makes you go crazy and then kills you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to see the [redacted] stuff in the interview and murder descriptions removed. It's not making me think of anything to terrible to print. Murders can be described in clinical detail rather than gruesome language, and nothing makes an SCP less scary than hearing it say "I want to [REDACTED]"

That said, this one pays off in the end. I like the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was on the fence on this one, but I liked the recovery and the interview was chilling, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Imbecile" was originally a clinical term, but has not been so for decades. These days we refer to "retardation".

So, let me see if I understand the basic premise here. There's an entity living inside the tape. It feeds on the curiosity of people watching. If the curiosity is intense enough, it becomes able to manifest in the real world… at which point it kills them horribly, and then demanifests.

Is that it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad, but it's not particularly good either, as is. Some of the wording's a bit weird ( "…of imbecile intelligence.", "aforestated"). No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you mean eyeliner, not mascara.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think there's anything inherently *bad* with this one… It's decent, far from horrible. It just doesn't really have anything to stand out, and has a few small issues that have been touched on already. If you can clean this up a bit more, it might be enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a tape that makes you crazy and die. There are chunks where the tone is off and mascara isn't specifically feminine. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really on the fence over this one. It is interesting, and I like the idea behind it, but it really is a crazy tape of doom. I think I'll tentatively upvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed, this is pretty solid for a first article. Bits of it are pretty clunky, though, especially this paragaph:

Event 1757-SE climaxes only for those who become obsessed with the film, repeatedly watching it or trying to get others to watch it with them.

"climaxes" is weird usage and excessively dramatic. How about "occurs"?

The event culminates with a visit from a malevolent force

This is odd and not very clinical. If we've never seen it, attributing the wounds to a "malevolent force" which "visits" seems like anthropomorphization. Describe what happens without this kind of speculation.

At any rate, I like the interplay of the entity's curiosity and that of the viewers. Edit this aggressively and you've got my upvote. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing that you've done particularly wrong here- the tone is mostly fine, as is the execution- but ultimately it's just one more thing that makes you crazy and kills you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the record, you know some guys just wear mascara, right? It's not intrinsically feminine.

Otherwise, pretty good. I'm not completely won over by it enough to upvote it (weird turns of phrase; "aforestated" is just too clunky a word, even if it is technically correct English), but it's pretty good. I might change my mind and upvote it later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP, my first on the site. C&C away!
